STATEMENT THAT NO OPPOSITION WAS RECEIVED RE: MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS (ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION); REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLTF RAIF DENKTAS FILED BY DEFENDANT ROXUL, LLC LUXOR CABS, INC.
ORDER RE: MOTION TO COMPEL

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS ROXUL, LLC AND LUXOR CABS, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS (ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION); REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLTFFS RAIF DENKTAS - MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART. WITHIN 15 DAYS PLAINTIFF SHALL: (1) COMPLY WITH THE JANUARY 26, 2012 ORDER RE DISCOVERY MOTION; (2) PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS THAT WERE REQUESTED IN DEFENDANTS' RE-NOTICE OF TAKING DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF DATED JUNE 11, 2012; AND (3) SUBMIT TO ONE MORE DAY OF DEPOSITION BY DEFENDANTS. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS' RIGHT TO SEEK FURTHER RELIEF FROM THE COURT, INCLUDING TERMINATING SANCTIONS, IN THE EVENT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER. SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,034.00 IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: ERNST HALPERIN CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

DISCOVERY 302, A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ROXUL, LLC, LUXOR CABS, INC.'S MOTION AND SECOND MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS ATTORNEY IS DENIED IN PART. AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING AS TO WHY THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED IN THE EVENT THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH THE PRIOR DISCOVERY ORDER IS SET FOR DECEMBER 7, 2012. MS. STOREY IS TO PREPARE THE ORDER. ERNST HALPERIN, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RAIF DENKTAS' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 21, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE COUNSEL AN OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. COUNSEL MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 13, 2012 AT 4:00 PM, OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. DISCOVERY 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: TERMINATING SANCTIONS - THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT, AT HEARING, DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL PROVIDES PROOF (E.G., A FEDEX SHIPPING LABEL OR OTHER SUCH DOCUMENTATION) THAT THE NOTICE OF NEW HEARING AND ASSOCIATED PAPERS WERE IN FACT SERVED ON PLAINTIFF AT HIS LAST KNOWN ADDRESS ON DECEMBER 7, 2012, AND THAT THE LAS VEGAS ADDRESS INDICATED IN THE PROOF OF SERVICE IS IN FACT PLAINTIFF'S LAST KNOWN ADDRESS. PROVIDED THAT THOSE CONDITIONS ARE MET, PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT WILL BE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE, WITH DEFENDANTS TO RECOVER COSTS, AND THE COURT WILL ENTER A SEPARATE JUDGMENT ACCORDINGLY IN A FORM TO BE PREPARED BY DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: JIM WEIXEL, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS ROXUL, LLC AND LUXOR CABS, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM JUDGE BRAD KAPLAN IN ITS ENTIRETY. THAT RECOMMENDATION IS TO A) GRANT THE MOTION TO COMPEL AS TO THE SPECIAL AND FORM INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT REQUESTS AND REQUIRE THE PLAINTIFF TO SERVE OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES AND TO PRODUCE THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WITHIN TWENTY DAYS, B) DENY THE MOTION TO HAVE FACTS DEEMED ADMITTED, BUT TO REQUIRE PLAINTIFF TO SERVE OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES WITHIN TWENTY DAYS; AND C) DENY THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

Order To Show Cause Re: Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - RAIF B. DENKTAS, TEL. NO. (510) 860-5037, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JOANNA L. STOREY OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Re: Terminating Sanctions - The motion for terminating sanctions is granted on the condition that, at hearing, Defendants' counsel provides proof (e.g., a FedEx shipping label or other such documentation) that the Notice of New Hearing and associated papers were in fact served on Plaintiff at his last known address on December 7, 2012, and that the Las Vegas address indicated in the proof of service is in fact Plaintiff's last known address. Provided that those conditions are met, Plaintiff's complaint will be dismissed with prejudice, with Defendants to recover costs, and the Court will enter a separate judgment accordingly in a form to be prepared by Defendants' counsel. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Jim Weixel, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt

Order To Show Cause Re: Terminating Sanctions

MINI MINUTES:

Joanna L. Storey, Hassard Bonnington, 415.288.9800, appearing for the Defendant;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Ruling: the Court continued the Order To Show Cause Re: Terminating Sanctions to December 19, 2012. Ms. Storey is to reissue the notice of hearing, give notice at the last known address, and serve by Fed Ex. Nils Rosenquest, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Second Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Agaisnt Pltf And His Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jennifer Nicoletto, 415.368.1810, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Joanna Storey, Hassard Bonnington, 415.288.9800, appearing for the Defendant.

Ruling: A R G U E D; DEFENDANT ROXUL, LLC, LUXOR CABS, INC.'S Motion And Second Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney is DENIED IN PART. The Court sets a hearing for December 7, 2012 at 9:00 for an order to show cause why terminating sanctions should not be granted in the event that plaintiff has not complied with the prior discovery order. Ms. Storey is to prepare the order. Ernst Halperin, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT

ATTY. JOANNA L. STOREY OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS ROXUL, LLC AND LUXOR CABS, INCORPORATED'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions (Alternative Motion To Compel Further Responses And Document Production); Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltffs Raif Denktas - MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART. Within 15 days Plaintiff shall: (1) comply with the January 26, 2012 Order re Discovery Motion; (2) produce all documents that were requested in Defendants' Re-Notice of Taking Deposition and Request for Production of Documents to Plaintiff dated June 11, 2012; and (3) submit to one more day of deposition by Defendants. This order is without prejudice to Defendants' right to seek further relief from the court, including terminating sanctions, in the event Plaintiff does not comply with this order. SANCTION IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,034.00 IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS ROXUL, LLC AND LUXOR CABS, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan in its entirety. That recommendation is to a) grant the motion to compel as to the special and form interrogatories and document requests and require the plaintiff to serve objection-free responses and to produce the requested documents within twenty days, b) deny the motion to have facts deemed admitted, but to require plaintiff to serve objection-free responses within twenty days; and c) deny the request for sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/HEK)
